evals.name: setup-react-project
evals.prompt: Set up Figma Code Connect in my React project. Here's our design system file: https://figma.com/design/abc123/DesignSystem?node-id=0-1
evals.expected_output: Response describes a complete setup workflow: checking MCP authentication, detecting React as the framework, installing @figma/code-connect, creating figma.config.json, and optionally generating design system rules.

assertions.text: Response describes checking Figma MCP authentication (e.g., via whoami) as a prerequisite step before proceeding
Response describes detecting the project framework (React) from package.json or project files
Response includes installing or verifying @figma/code-connect CLI availability as part of the setup workflow
Response describes creating or configuring figma.config.json with appropriate settings for the project
Response mentions generating design system rules (e.g., via create_design_system_rules) to establish project conventions

evals.name: design-to-code-with-existing-connect
evals.prompt: Implement this card component from our Figma: https://figma.com/design/xyz789/App?node-id=42-156. We already have Code Connect set up.
evals.expected_output: Response describes parsing the Figma URL to extract fileKey and nodeId, checking existing Code Connect mappings, retrieving design context, and adapting output to the project's stack and conventions.

assertions.text: Response identifies the need to extract fileKey and nodeId from the Figma URL, converting the dash-separated node-id to colon format (e.g., 42-156 becomes 42:156)
Response describes checking for existing Code Connect mappings (e.g., via get_code_connect_map) before generating new code
Response describes retrieving design context from Figma (e.g., via get_design_context) using the extracted file key and node ID
Response emphasizes adapting generated code to the project's existing components, tokens, and conventions rather than using raw Figma output

evals.name: design-to-code-no-connect
evals.prompt: Build this hero section from Figma: https://figma.com/design/def456/Marketing?node-id=10-200. We don't have Code Connect yet.
evals.expected_output: Response describes getting a visual reference and design context from Figma, gracefully handling the absence of Code Connect, generating adapted code, and recommending Code Connect setup afterward.

assertions.text: Response mentions getting a visual reference or screenshot of the Figma design to guide implementation
Response acknowledges the absence of Code Connect and describes proceeding with design-to-code without requiring it
Response describes generating implementation code adapted to the project's framework and conventions
Response recommends setting up Code Connect after implementation to improve future design-to-code workflows

evals.name: publish-code-connect-mappings
evals.prompt: Publish Code Connect for our Button component at src/components/Button.tsx to the Figma file https://figma.com/design/abc123/DS?node-id=5-30
evals.expected_output: Response describes getting AI-suggested mappings, generating a .figma.tsx file with prop mappings, validating with CLI parse, publishing via CLI, and verifying the result.

assertions.text: Response describes using AI-suggested mappings (e.g., via get_code_connect_suggestions) to guide the component mapping process
Response describes generating a .figma.tsx Code Connect file with prop mappings for the Button component
Response includes a validation step (e.g., figma connect parse) before publishing the mapping
Response describes publishing the Code Connect mapping to Figma Dev Mode via the CLI
Response includes a verification step after publishing to confirm the mapping was registered in Figma

evals.name: publish-dry-run
evals.prompt: I want to test my Code Connect mappings before actually publishing. Can you do a dry run for https://figma.com/design/abc123/DS?node-id=5-30?
evals.expected_output: Response describes running a dry-run publish and presenting the results without actually publishing.

assertions.text: Response mentions using the --dry-run flag with the Code Connect publish command
Response does not describe running an actual publish without the --dry-run flag
Response describes presenting the dry-run output to the user showing what would be published

evals.name: extract-tokens-css-variables
evals.prompt: Extract the design tokens from our Figma file https://figma.com/design/tok123/Tokens?node-id=0-1 and generate CSS custom properties. We use a tokens/ directory.
evals.expected_output: Response describes extracting Figma variables, detecting CSS output format, generating CSS custom properties organized by category, and writing to the tokens/ directory.

assertions.text: Response describes extracting Figma variables (e.g., via get_variable_defs) from the provided design file
Response specifies outputting tokens as CSS custom properties (--variable-name: value format) rather than raw JSON
Response references the user's specified tokens/ directory as the output location for generated token files
Response describes organizing tokens by category (e.g., colors, spacing, typography) in the output

evals.name: extract-tokens-tailwind
evals.prompt: Pull our design tokens from Figma and add them to our Tailwind config. Figma file: https://figma.com/design/tok123/Tokens?node-id=0-1
evals.expected_output: Response describes extracting Figma variables, detecting Tailwind in the project, and mapping tokens to Tailwind theme.extend format.

assertions.text: Response describes detecting Tailwind CSS in the project (e.g., via tailwind.config.js or postcss config)
Response specifies outputting tokens in Tailwind theme.extend format rather than generic CSS custom properties
Response describes extracting Figma variables (e.g., via get_variable_defs) as the source for design token data

evals.name: roundtrip-full-lifecycle
evals.prompt: We're starting a new project and want the full Figma-to-code setup. Our design system is at https://figma.com/design/abc123/DS?node-id=0-1. We use React with Tailwind.
evals.expected_output: Response describes the complete roundtrip workflow: setup (auth, framework, CLI, config), token extraction, design-to-code generation, Code Connect publishing, and verification.

assertions.text: Response begins with authentication and framework detection as the initial setup phase
Response includes a token extraction step that describes pulling design variables from Figma into the project
Response includes a design-to-code step describing component implementation from the design system
Response includes a step describing publishing Code Connect mappings back to Figma Dev Mode
Response includes a verification step to confirm the full roundtrip pipeline is working end-to-end

evals.name: error-auth-failure-mcp
evals.expected_output: Response describes checking authentication first, explains how to handle auth failures via OAuth, and does not proceed with design operations without valid auth.

assertions.text: Response describes checking Figma authentication (e.g., via whoami) as the very first step before any design operations
Response explains how to resolve authentication failures, including the Figma MCP OAuth flow or token setup
Response does not describe proceeding with design context retrieval or other Figma operations without first verifying authentication

evals.name: error-cli-not-installed
evals.prompt: Publish my Code Connect mappings for the Button component to Figma.
evals.expected_output: Response describes checking CLI availability first and providing installation guidance if the CLI is not found.

assertions.text: Response describes checking whether the @figma/code-connect CLI is available before attempting to publish
Response provides installation instructions for @figma/code-connect (e.g., npm install -D) if the CLI is not found
Response does not describe attempting to publish without first confirming CLI availability

evals.name: branch-url-handling
evals.prompt: Implement this from our feature branch design: https://figma.com/design/abc123/branch/branchKey456/MyFile?node-id=8-99
evals.expected_output: Response correctly identifies the branch URL format and describes using branchKey456 as the fileKey with nodeId 8:99.

assertions.text: Response correctly identifies the branch URL format and specifies using branchKey456 as the fileKey (not abc123)
Response extracts the nodeId as 8:99 (converting the dash in node-id to a colon)
Response describes retrieving design context using branchKey456 as the fileKey and 8:99 as the nodeId

evals.name: vue-framework-detection
evals.prompt: Set up Figma Code Connect for my Vue 3 project and implement the NavBar component from https://figma.com/design/vue123/App?node-id=3-15
evals.expected_output: Response identifies Vue 3 as the framework, specifies 'Vue' as the Code Connect label, describes generating Vue SFC format code, and mentions configuring the client framework as vue.

assertions.text: Response identifies Vue 3 as the project framework based on the user's description or project configuration
Response specifies using 'Vue' as the Code Connect label when configuring or creating mappings
Response describes generating code in Vue Single File Component format (template, script setup, style sections) rather than React JSX
Response mentions configuring the client framework as 'vue' when interacting with Figma tools or Code Connect settings
